data.title: Jack in the Box offers $5 Smashed Jacks for 7 days, costing $35 in total, named "Best Burger in Fast Food."
data.description: In San Diego, Jack in the Box is offering a special deal during Burger Week from September 12-18. Customers can enjoy the popular Smashed Jack burger for only $5 each day, totaling $35 for seven days, both in-store and on the Jack App. The Smashed Jack, hailed as the "Best Burger in Fast Food," was first introduced in 2024 and has become a fan favorite. This promotion allows fans to indulge in seven days of delicious smashed perfection at an affordable price.

data.title: Hain Celestial Group to report EPS of $0.03 on 2025-09-15. Analysts expect company to surpass estimates. Stock trading at $2.12. Neutral rating, $2.5 target price. 74.25% decline in 52-week period.

Investors are hopeful for positive news that exceeds expectations and provides optimistic guidance for the next quarter, as this can influence stock prices significantly. data.title: Copper Mountain Tech unveils new affordable VNAs: VTR0102 covers 50 MHz to 1.5 GHz, VTR0302 spans 50 MHz to 3.5 GHz.
data.description: A new line of vector network analyzers has been unveiled by Copper Mountain Technologies in Indianapolis on September 12, 2025. The launch includes the 2-port VTR0102 with a frequency range of 50 MHz to 1.5 GHz and the 2-port VTR0302 with a frequency range of 50 MHz to 3.5 GHz. These cost-effective VNAs are specifically tailored for standard S11 and S21 measurements and cater to a variety of applications.

TD: DOLLAR COULD GAIN IF FED STAYS CAUTIOUS TD Securities says the dollar may rise if the Fed delivers a 25bp cut next week but signals caution on further easing, stressing inflation risks. Markets expect back-to-back cuts, but Powell could emphasize a data-dependent approach.

data.title: RH reported Q2 earnings of $2.93/share, missing estimate of $3.20/share. Revenue $899.15M, below $904.64M estimate. CEO cites industry-leading growth.
data.description: RH (NYSE: RH) announced their second-quarter results, which fell below expectations. The company reported earnings of $2.93 per share, missing the estimated $3.20 per share. Their quarterly revenue was $899.15 million, below the expected $904.64 million. Despite challenges such as tariff uncertainty and a tough housing market, RH still achieved significant growth with an 8.4% increase in revenue and a 13.7% rise in demand. CEO Gary Friedman acknowledged these obstacles but highlighted the company's strong performance. RH also adjusted its fiscal 2025 revenue forecast. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Adobe beats Q3 expectations with $5.31 EPS and $5.99B revenue; raises fiscal 2025 outlook. CEO states AI creative applications leading. Adjusted EPS guidance raised to $20.80-$20.85 for 2025.
data.description: Adobe Inc. (NASDAQ: ADBE) exceeded expectations in the third quarter and revised its fiscal 2025 outlook. The company reported earnings of $5.31 per share, surpassing the expected $5.18 per share. Quarterly revenue stood at $5.99 billion, beating the estimated $5.91 billion. Adobe's AI creative applications segment demonstrated significant growth, with AI-influenced Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) surpassing $5 billion and AI-first ARR exceeding the year-end target of $250 million. Shantanu Narayen, Adobe's chair and CEO, highlighted the company's leadership in the AI creative applications category. The revised fiscal 2025 adjusted EPS guidance is now between $20.80 and $20.85.
